codeReview.summary: The current implementation closes both prior blocking gaps and now satisfies the import-surface assertions tied to this feature. `use net::http` imports are parsed as first-class syntax, qualified imported types such as `http::Request` now reach import-aware diagnostics in type position, imported aliases are reserved across the contracted top-level item kinds including traits, and imported-package member lookup yields member-aware errors instead of unrelated local-name failures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `compiler-worker` skill still says to write tests first, but this fix session edited production code before adding the new regression tests and the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`. The skill guidance or the handoff expectation should be clarified so scrutiny can treat `followedProcedure` as reliable evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md:22` says "Write tests first". The latest transcript skeleton for worker session `64010c1a-d6a6-44dc-b8c8-334561b28747` shows an edit to `lib/resolver.ml` before later edits to `test/test_resolver.ml` and `test/e2e/test_e2e.ml`, while the handoff JSON reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Pass. The prior failures are fixed: `parser.mly` now parses `pkg::Type` in type position, `resolver.ml` raises missing-import diagnostics for `TyPath`, trait declarations now participate in import-alias collision checks, and the repo has focused parser/resolver/typecheck/e2e coverage for VAL-IMPORT-004 through VAL-IMPORT-008. I did not find any new blocking issues in the current implementation for this feature.
